title: Investigate temporal-worker restart root cause from logs

log.message: Created from Congress #58 verdict on: [idea]: service temporal-worker.service restarted 4x in last 24h — possible flakiness (GitHub issue:

Source verdict: ABORTED by Ibrahim: This is an operational investigation, not a policy question. Four restarts is a datum that requires reading `journalctl --user -u temporal-worker.service` and classifying the failu...

Found root cause: reminder_wf.py line 22 used datetime.now(timezone.utc) inside a Temporal workflow, violating the determinism sandbox. This caused RestrictedWorkflowAccessError on every OnceReminderWorkflow activation. Fixed by replacing with workflow.now().

Note: the ~hourly service restarts are intentional (simplify cron pushes code changes, claude-bot restarts worker). These are NOT crashes. The reminder_wf bug caused workflow-level failures, not service crashes.

Fixed reminder_wf.py: replaced datetime.now(timezone.utc) with workflow.now() and removed unused timezone import. Committed cbcf363, pushed to GitHub, restarted temporal-worker.service — confirmed healthy. Service restarts were intentional (simplify cron), not crashes.

Root cause: reminder_wf.py used datetime.now() inside workflow (non-deterministic, banned by Temporal sandbox). Fixed: replaced with workflow.now(), removed timezone import. Commit cbcf363. Service healthy.
